summary: `shape`: a predicate over a closed enum of engine-implemented, doc-cited shapes, opening with the two documented ones — the skill name's hyphen rules and no-XML-tags-in-description

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (132) gains `Shape { field, shape }` over a new closed `Shape` enum whose members are engine-implemented and doc-cited, opening with exactly two: the skill name's hyphen rules (no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen) and no-XML-tags-in-description. An unknown shape is rejected at load exactly as an unknown predicate is, never skipped (`contract.md`, "clause": the vocabulary is closed and growing it is a deliberate language change) — `predicate_from_row` (395) is that boundary, and its `"type"` arm (408-415) is the standing precedent for decoding a named argument into an engine enum, an unknown name yielding no predicate at all; the arm's own comment (403-407) spells the exact rule this entry follows — an unknown name is no predicate at all, while a vacuous-but-decodable argument fails admissibility instead. Carrying a `field`, it joins three field-listing matches beside `AllowedChars`: `key()` (534), `target()` (595), `documented_field()` (638). No author ever writes a pattern: 0033 refuses the author-facing pattern clause for the third time on the record. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; 7fae62e moved the decoder and the three matches ~+9 to ~+13 while the enum head held at 132.

Each shape's mechanics are `regex`, hidden exactly as it hides under `allowed_chars` (813). The violation message names the shape and the clause's guidance teaches it — `declared_kinds` (981) is the shipped precedent for rendering a predicate's declared argument into its finding's prose. `admissibility` (110) needs no new refusal for an unknown shape — it never decodes into a clause at all. **The load-bearing rider, and the compiler will not catch it**: a `shape` clause's `field` is an addressing path like every other value predicate's, so it joins `addressed_field` (197, its list 199-210) — the list `unaddressable` (176) reads to parse and fence a path. That match ends in a catch-all `_ => None` (211), so omitting the arm compiles clean and silently leaves a shape clause's path unparsed and un-fenced — the subset bound going unenforced on one predicate, with no build error to say so. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; 7fae62e moved every address ~+8 to ~+61 while `admissibility` held at 110.

`ClauseRow` (2704) gains a `shape` column carrying the enum member's name, following the `value_type` precedent (2767, written at 3628-3629 and read at 3673): the row family carries the argument as its name and decodes it at the boundary rather than carrying an engine type. Follow the **name-carrying** half, not the arity — `value_type` is `Option<Vec<String>>` with a read-side bare-string tolerance (`opt_str_or_str_array`, 3355, its doc at 3349) because a set widened under it. A shape is one name, so the column is a plain `Option<String>` and needs no skew tolerance of its own. Every address re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window (`git diff` empty), so all stand — and note the window's own widening is the counter-case, not the precedent: `closed-keys` names no argument and took no column at all.

`clause_from_row` (216) and the three `value_type: None` row-build sites (273, 301, 324, all inside the file's own `mod tests` at 252) carry the column. All re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

The exhaustive `match` (83) needs an arm, and which one is a real choice the entry must make and state: emitting a shape's hidden regex as JSON Schema's `pattern` puts a pattern in front of an author through the back door, while the no-op arm list (135-154) leaves the keystroke quieter than the gate. `distribution.md` ("The placements and their enforcement modes") bounds it — the generated schema is the decidable predicates only, and it is machine output, never clause surface. `addresses_a_property` (222) is the freshest reading of that bound and worth citing whichever way this lands: it suppresses a nested-path clause from the schema outright rather than forging a keystroke finding the validation channel cannot honour. 7fae62e sharpened the same rule from the other side — its closed-schema emission (178-196) names a declared key as an empty subschema precisely so the editor never squiggles a key the contract declares. A forged keystroke error is the one thing this channel may not emit, whichever arm this lands in. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the window moved every address ~+3 to ~+23.

A `shape()` ctor and a `Shape` string-union type join the node-scope predicates (the block from 83, whose header comment 84-86 the window rewrote to name the key-naming and fieldless exceptions `closedKeys` (127) added). An author picks a named shape and can spell nothing else — the closed enum is the type. The `type` ctor (95-101) is the shipped precedent for an SDK ctor whose argument is the closed lattice's own spelling. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; 7fae62e moved the ctor ~+2.

`skillDefaultContract`'s header (1058-1061) names both holds and already names this widening ("a narrow shape predicate governs additions"); both discharge — the name's hyphen rules and the platform's "no XML tags in the description" become clauses with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The `allowedChars` name clause (1082) already keeps XML out of the *name* and its guidance says so; this is the description's own bar, and the two must not double-cover the name. **The cross-reference rider changed hands in the window and now rides this entry alone**: 7fae62e discharged `pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s hold outright — its header (601-631) now reads "The whole profile ships … nothing decidable is held" — which leaves `marketplaceDefaultContract`'s header (958-960) pointing at "the same shape of hold `pluginManifestDefaultContract` names for its own two", a sibling that today names none. Retiring the skill's two here makes that sentence's last true reading impossible, so re-word it to name the widening the marketplace's own hold waits on, never a sibling's count. Every address re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the window moved the skill header ~+122 and left the marketplace sentence untouched-but-falsified.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit. Untouched in the window; `closed-keys` needed no column of its own.
Generated data — re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (142) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so `Shape` is a compile error until it lands in an arm. It belongs in the no-op list (165-185) — the oracle measures the plugin-manifest floor against the published schema, this widening ships clauses on `skill` alone, and the schema states no shape rule for a `type`/`minLength`-vocabulary diff to count. `EXPECTED_LAG` (40) must not move; if it does, the arm is wrong. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb: 7fae62e landed `ClosedKeys` in that very no-op list (169) with `EXPECTED_LAG` unmoved — the precedent for this arm is one entry old, and its own comment (157-164) is the shape the shape arm's rationale follows.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `shape` column carrying the declared shape's enum name, when the predicate is `shape`.

tests.asserts: a skill name with a leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen is a finding and a clean slug is not; a description carrying an XML tag is a finding; a lock row naming a shape the enum does not carry is refused at load rather than skipped; no clause surface accepts an author-written pattern

acceptance: The two documented decidable spec rules the skill contract has named as a hold since it shipped are gated, over a closed enum no author can extend (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's fourth widening — the "later wave under their own design" 0022 reserved. The enum's closure is the whole safety property: it separates a shape from the open lint `intent.md` invariant 2 exists to prevent, so a shape lands with its doc cite or it does not land. Gate opened at 0c3cbcb: CLOSED-KEYS-CLAUSE shipped and left the queue, so nothing serializes this over the five files the two shared. scoped 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 0c3cbcb.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at — deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own — its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb — the file is untouched in the window and the roster still carries eleven.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone).

The root export carries the dial. 7fae62e edited this file — one line, `closedKeys` joining the predicate-ctor export block (29) — so the dial's export lands beside a block the window just moved, and this is the file that serializes the entry behind SHAPE-PREDICATE only via `sdk/src/builtins.ts`.

The dial's document face is ruled (0034): a local-locus `toml-document`. **Every half is upstream code on disk by the time this is picked** — `Commitment::Local` (46-56) and `CustomKind::local` (735) shipped at 40619a0, the layout-only arm that would otherwise refuse this kind on contact is retired (bce89b7, `local_locus_fault` at 750 now fencing the `governs`-less locus alone), `Format::TomlDocument` (568, its name at 580 and its decode at 975) shipped at 09ef5ea as a read face with no write twin — exactly what a never-projected local document needs — and 6e7b958 made the walk find the dial's own `.temper/dial.toml` at all, which is what keeps this kind's rows from deriving over an empty set. All addresses re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window. This entry declares the kind; it mints no format and no locus mechanics of its own.

Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode — so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass. b753358 reshaped this file around the lock family, and the dial rides that shape rather than minting a second one: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) is the one read of every layered input, before any consumer, and returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) whose `joined_clauses` field is where a policy layer's clauses already sit. Read it before adding a severity-application seam. Addresses re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

schemaDelta: none of the lock's own — the dial is a shipped kind declaring a local locus over the `toml-document` format, and a local member's rows never enter the lock, deriving at read time instead.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it — locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Un-forked at 832f015: 0034 ruled `(local-locus-toml-face)` whole — the dial stays local-locus TOML, and "layout-only" retires as the wrong fence. Serialized only, behind SHAPE-PREDICATE over `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. All three 0034 derivations have shipped (bce89b7, 09ef5ea, 6e7b958), so nothing upstream is unbuilt — only the chain's file serialization holds it. scoped f1d97e4; cites re-read at 0c3cbcb.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock — the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional — a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict. Two of the three are already assembled and need no re-derivation: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) carrying both the local-locus read-time rows and `joined_clauses`, read once before any consumer. The joined third is the live gap b753358 named and deferred here by name: it shipped the join with every finding carrying its layer in the address (`<label>@<layer>`, `qualify_layer_label` at 1546), so a layer that produces no finding gates silently today — exactly what this closes. Addresses re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line. All three re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five — it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. The joined-lock third is **built and unannounced** (b753358), the first of the three to reach that state; scope is unchanged, and the commit body should say so. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`). scoped f1d97e4; cites re-read at 0c3cbcb.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). Every address re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1349) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1350-1354) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1352) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. Re-read on disk at 0c3cbcb; the file is untouched in the window.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0c3cbcb and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. The window touched neither file, so every address carries unmoved from last tick's re-read rather than being re-derived. The constant is still 5 on disk (`graph.rs`:59), so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; park re-tested 0c3cbcb.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0c3cbcb, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 385c429..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
